text.title: AWS IAM Practical Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every application of `AWS IAM Practical Patterns` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Human access MUST come through IAM Identity Center permission sets, not direct IAM users. Direct IAM users are reserved for service identities with documented justification.

rationale.source: AWS Identity Center; CIS AWS Benchmark

rule.rationale: Direct IAM users with long-lived keys are the leaked-credentials class; SSO + STS scoped tokens close that.

rule.statement: Developer / engineer permission sets MUST have a Permissions Boundary capping max effective permissions, even if attached policies are temporarily broadened.
rule.rationale: Without boundary, a temporary `*` policy attachment becomes effective `*`; boundary is the org-level safety net.

rule.statement: Org-level SCP MUST deny: leaving the org, disabling CloudTrail, disabling GuardDuty, modifying root credentials, S3 public buckets in regulated accounts. SCPs MUST be tested against a canary OU before prod rollout.

rationale.source: AWS Organizations SCP docs; CIS AWS Benchmark

rule.rationale: Account-level IAM cannot prevent destructive actions if root or admin is compromised; SCP is the only org-level guardrail.

rule.statement: Sensitive actions (delete, key rotation, security-config changes) MUST require MFA condition (`aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent`) + source-IP or VPC condition where applicable.

rationale.source: AWS IAM Conditions docs; SOC2 audit findings

rule.rationale: Phished credentials without MFA = full access; MFA condition + IP-pinning shrinks blast radius.

rule.statement: IAM Access Analyzer MUST be enabled in every account; findings MUST be reviewed within 7 days with named owner + decision (`fix | accepted-with-rationale | false-positive`).
rule.rationale: Access Analyzer surfaces external-shared resources; un-triaged findings hide real data-exposure.

rule.statement: STS / SSO session tags MUST propagate user identity into CloudTrail (via `sts:SetSourceIdentity` or session_name = email) for action attribution.
rule.rationale: Shared-role audit trails read `AssumedRole/X/Y` with no human attribution; session tags restore traceability.

rule.statement: AWS-managed `AdministratorAccess` policy MUST NOT be attached to human roles; use custom least-privilege permission sets with quarterly review.
rule.rationale: AdministratorAccess on humans nullifies every other control; quarterly-reviewed custom policies are the only defensible baseline.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
